mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in positive direction over 3-year rolling windows, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear regimes with a t-stat > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut margins and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in >60% of tightening periods, or regime interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS slope is flat.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 200,000 per month confirms economic resilience, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows or positive returns occur in <50% of high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production indicates inventory drawdowns rather than demand weakness, leading to restocking cycles and positive quarterly market returns.
failure_condition: Divergence signals yield negative returns in >60% of cases, or the spread loses predictive power when volatility indices exceed 25.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration above 3% annualized during an economic expansion forces central banks to prioritize price stability, triggering rate hike expectations that compress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation expansion months, or the signal fails to predict direction when real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production index declines directly reduce near-term corporate output expectations, prompting algorithmic selling and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal generates positive returns in >58% of declining months, or autocorrelation in returns explains >80% of the predictive variance.
